title: CXMT Is Growing Fast, But Can’t Solve The DRAM Shortage
text:
CXMT Is Growing Fast, But Can’t Solve The DRAM Shortage

Laila Khawaja

Gavekal Technologies: Chips | 2 Jul 2026

Changxin Memory Technologies' planned US$4–5bn Shanghai STAR Market IPO has raised concerns that China’s DRAM champion could flood the market with excess capacity and aggressive prices, but that risk looks overstated in the near term. Most of its output is being absorbed domestically, and its capacity ramp is unlikely to provide meaningful global relief before 2028. CXMT remains focused on conventional DRAM, benefiting as Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron shift capacity toward high-bandwidth memory, which has tightened conventional DRAM supply. Its HBM ambitions are credible but constrained by export controls and execution risk, while any slowdown in AI-driven demand could expose CXMT if the big three redirect capacity back to conventional DRAM, where CXMT still lags on cost, yield, and process technology.
